steps.title: Identify how both texts prove authorship
steps.content:
Both Sykes and modern scholars identify Fletcher's authorship through style analysis.

Sykes explicitly states that Fletcher's “distinct style in his other plays” is “sufficient evidence of Fletcher's authorship.” Modern scholars similarly determine authorship “on the basis of style,” attributing certain acts to Fletcher.

steps.title: Match the shared assumption to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A states that Fletcher's writing has a unique, identifiable style—the shared premise both texts build on.

For Sykes, Fletcher's distinct style proved authorship; for modern scholars, style analysis determined which acts he wrote.

The other choices are either contradicted (C: Sykes disputes Shakespeare's role), completely unsupported (D), or unique to one text's argument (B: women depictions appear only in Sykes's reasoning, not Text 2's style-based approach).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
